D7 Print L4 Analyze Reference

i1 Spectrophotometer Readout

a handheld probe shines on a patch, reads its reflectance every few nanometres, integrates against the standard observer, and reports an L*a*b* — and a ΔE to target

A spectrophotometer like the X-Rite i1 measures the reflectance spectrum of a printed patch from ~380 to 730 nm, then integrates against the CIE colour-matching functions and a chosen illuminant to produce X, Y, Z, and from there an L*a*b*. Subtract a reference patch's Lab and you get a ΔE — the number printers live and die by.

Step the probe across the test strip. For each patch the spectrum updates, the measured L*a*b* refreshes, and the ΔE to its ISO reference is compared against the standard's tolerance — easy to see which patches passed and which broke.
